Title: Effects of elevated [CO2] on maize defence against mycotoxigenic Fusarium verticillioides

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Jasmonic acid; fumonisin-associated host oxylipin signals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 dampened pathogen-induced LOX1 transcription, consistent with weakened oxylipin defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course after Fusarium verticillioides inoculation; metabolite profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in maize stems after Fusarium verticillioides inoculation; lower induction at elevated CO2, especially at 60 min
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX1 transcripts increased after fungal inoculation. 2. LOX1 induction was 73% lower at elevated CO2 at 60 min. 3. Because LOX1 has both 9-LOX and 13-LOX functionality, its attenuation is consistent with reduced defence oxylipin signalling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies oxylipin branch signals affecting downstream defence metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated atmospheric CO2 (800 vs 400 µmol mol−1 air); Fusarium verticillioides inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as having both 9-LOX and 13-LOX functionality; exact terminal product assignment not directly resolved in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to form hydroperoxy oxylipin precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defence signalling affecting resistance to fungal pathogen and fumonisin-related interaction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Attenuation correlated with increased susceptibility to fungal growth

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced induction of LOX3 at elevated CO2 coincided with lower fumonisin production per unit fungal biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course after Fusarium verticillioides inoculation; correlation with fumonisin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fusarium-induced in stems; transcript levels approximately 2.5-fold higher at ambient CO2 than elevated CO2 at 120 min
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX3 is a pathogen-induced 9-LOX gene. 2. Its induction was attenuated at elevated CO2. 3. Reduced LOX3 induction is discussed as consistent with reduced fumonisin per unit fungal biomass.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies host 9-LOX oxylipin signals proposed to stimulate fungal fumonisin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated atmospheric CO2; Fusarium verticillioides inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion cites prior lox3 mutant work showing reduced fumonisin without impairing fungal growth; this paper itself provides transcriptional correlation rather than mutant validation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for host-derived fumonisin-stimulatory signal, inferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces oxygen into polyunsaturated fatty acids in the 9-LOX branch to generate oxylipin precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Host oxylipin signalling influencing fungal mycotoxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower LOX3 signalling may reduce fumonisin per unit fungus but accompanies higher host susceptibility

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 reduced pathogen-induced LOX4 transcription, consistent with dampened defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Fusarium inoculation; lower at one or more time points under elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX4 is induced by Fusarium infection. 2. Elevated CO2 dampens its induction. 3. This attenuation contributes to weakened maize oxylipin defence responses.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Individual metabolite products not directly measured for LOX4.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids in the 9-LOX branch.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 reduced inducible LOX5 transcription, consistent with increased susceptibility to Fusarium verticillioides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; literature-supported interpretation cited in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Fusarium in stems; attenuated at elevated CO2 at one or more time points
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX5 is part of the maize defence-associated LOX response. 2. Elevated CO2 suppresses its induction after pathogen attack. 3. Reduced LOX5 signalling is consistent with compromised resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion cites prior disruption studies linking LOX5 activity to resistance, but this paper measures transcripts only.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to produce oxylipin precursors.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pathogen-induced LOX6 transcription was dampened at elevated CO2, consistent with reduced defence response.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Fusarium inoculation; lower at one or more time points under elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX6 responds transcriptionally to Fusarium challenge. 2. Elevated CO2 reduces its induction. 3. This fits the global attenuation of maize LOX-dependent defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional output inferred from LOX family role rather than direct enzymology in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to oxylipin precursors.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 strongly reduced pathogen-induced LOX8 expression, consistent with reduced JA accumulation and downstream phytoalexin defence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course after Fusarium inoculation; JA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in infected stems at ambient CO2; 15-fold lower at elevated CO2 at 30 min
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX8 is directly involved in providing substrate for JA biosynthesis. 2. Its induction after Fusarium inoculation is strongly attenuated at elevated CO2. 3. Reduced LOX8 expression correlates with reduced JA and reduced terpenoid phytoalexins.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly notes direct involvement in production of substrate for JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts linolenic acid to 13-hydroperoxy linolenic acid, providing substrate for JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed entry to JA branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonate-mediated defence against fungal infection and induction of phytoalexins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced LOX8 activity/expression is associated with increased susceptibility

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of LOX9 was dampened at elevated CO2, consistent with reduced 13-LOX signalling during defence.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen-induced in stems; reduced at one or more time points at elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX9 belongs to the 13-LOX group with putative JA-related function. 2. Elevated CO2 suppresses its induction after Fusarium infection. 3. This contributes to reduced 13-LOX defence output.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool to JA-related oxylipin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Putative role in JA production cited; specific product not directly measured.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates linolenic acid in the 13-LOX branch.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Unlike most LOXs, LOX10 induction was stronger under elevated CO2 after pathogen inoculation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Fusarium inoculation; transcript levels 3-fold and 5-fold higher at elevated CO2 at 30 and 60 min
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX10 was the only LOX with stronger induction at elevated CO2. 2. It has been implicated in green leaf volatile production. 3. Its contrasting response highlights functional divergence within the LOX family.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Side-route oxylipin/volatile branch rather than main JA-defence output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes implication in green leaf volatile production, not direct JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroperoxy fatty acid precursors of green leaf volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates fatty acids in a branch implicated in green leaf volatile formation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pathogen-induced LOX11 transcription was reduced at elevated CO2, supporting weakened 13-LOX defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Fusarium inoculation; attenuated at one or more time points under elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX11 is among 13-LOXs with putative JA-related functionality. 2. Elevated CO2 reduces its induction. 3. This aligns with the dampened defence hormone response.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic role not directly validated in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates linolenic acid in the 13-LOX branch.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 reduced inducible LOX12 transcription, consistent with weakened defence-associated oxylipin signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fusarium-induced in stems; reduced at one or more time points under elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX12 is induced by pathogen challenge. 2. Its induction is dampened at elevated CO2. 3. The paper discusses LOX12 as contributing to resistance based on prior studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior disruption studies cited in discussion, but no direct genetic manipulation here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to form oxylipin precursors.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 reduced inducible LOX13 transcription, contributing to dampened 13-LOX signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Fusarium inoculation; attenuated at elevated CO2 at one or more time points
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX13 is among 13-LOXs with putative roles in JA production. 2. Elevated CO2 dampens its pathogen-induced expression. 3. This supports reduced JA-dependent defence output.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool to JA-related pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific biochemical role not individually resolved here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates linolenic acid in the 13-LOX branch.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOS transcripts increased after pathogen inoculation but were not significantly affected by elevated CO2.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced with time following Fusarium inoculation; similar at ambient and elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS is induced after pathogen infection. 2. Elevated CO2 does not significantly alter AOS transcript abundance. 3. JA suppression at elevated CO2 is therefore more closely associated with reduced LOX induction/substrate supply than with AOS transcription.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly assayed in Results as key JA biosynthetic gene.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 13-hydroperoxy linolenic acid to an unstable allene oxide intermediate in JA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOC transcripts increased following pathogen inoculation but were not significantly altered by elevated CO2.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced with time following Fusarium inoculation; similar at both CO2 levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC is pathogen inducible. 2. Elevated CO2 does not significantly change AOC transcription. 3. Reduced JA accumulation under elevated CO2 is not explained by altered AOC transcription.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly assayed in Results as key JA biosynthetic gene.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide intermediate to form the cyclopentenone precursor in JA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-mediated defence output; linolenic acid influx affecting jasmonic acid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CO2 altered NPR1 transcription from pathogen-induced to pathogen-repressed, consistent with compromised SA signalling and possibly reduced linolenic acid supply for JA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; SA profiling correlation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transcript level was 2.5-fold higher at elevated CO2 at time zero, but after Fusarium inoculation NPR1 was induced at ambient CO2 and down-regulated at elevated CO2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Basal NPR1 transcripts are higher under elevated CO2. 2. After Fusarium inoculation, NPR1 is induced at ambient CO2 but repressed at elevated CO2. 3. This inversion coincides with reduced SA induction and impaired defence signalling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator of SA-mediated systemic acquired resistance and potentially linolenic acid influx linked to JA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional control within defence signalling pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defence signalling; systemic acquired resistance; precursor supply for oxylipin signalling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes SA-dependent defence gene activation; discussed as required for activation mechanism controlling linolenic acid influx
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fusarium verticillioides infection; elevated atmospheric CO2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct target-gene assays performed here; role in linolenic acid influx is cited from prior work.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression under elevated CO2 correlates with increased susceptibility to Fusarium
